audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Sarah visitation, Isaac/laughter, weaning feast, Hagar and Ishmael wilderness episode, divine hearing, opened eyes/well, and Beersheba covenant.

verses.restored: The Eternal visited Sarah as he had said, and the Eternal did for Sarah as he had spoken.

familiar.text: And the LORD visited Sarah as he had said...

notes.title: Visited Sarah
notes.body: Paqad means visit, attend to, or act on behalf of. The promise centers Sarah directly.

verses.restored: Sarah conceived and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the appointed time of which the Divine had spoken to him.

familiar.text: For Sarah conceived, and bare Abraham a son in his old age...

notes.title: Appointed time
notes.body: Isaac is born at the promised moed, the appointed time.

verses.restored: Abraham called the name of his son who was born to him, whom Sarah bore to him, Isaac.

familiar.text: And Abraham called the name of his son... Isaac.

verses.restored: Abraham circumcised Isaac his son when he was eight days old, as the Divine had commanded him.

familiar.text: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac being eight days old...

verses.restored: Abraham was one hundred years old when Isaac his son was born to him.

familiar.text: And Abraham was an hundred years old...

verses.restored: Sarah said, The Divine has made laughter for me; everyone who hears will laugh with me.

familiar.text: And Sarah said, God hath made me to laugh...

notes.title: Laughter transformed
notes.body: Earlier laughter of impossibility becomes shared laughter of fulfillment.

verses.restored: She said, Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne a son in his old age.

familiar.text: And she said, Who would have said unto Abraham...

verses.restored: The child grew and was weaned, and Abraham made a great feast on the day Isaac was weaned.

familiar.text: And the child grew, and was weaned...

verses.restored: Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, laughing.

familiar.text: And Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian... mocking.

notes.title: Laughing/playing/mocking
notes.body: Metsacheq can mean laughing, playing, or mocking. The ambiguity matters because of Isaac’s laughter-name.

verses.restored: She said to Abraham, Drive out this slave-woman and her son, for the son of this slave-woman shall not inherit with my son, with Isaac.

familiar.text: Wherefore she said unto Abraham, Cast out this bondwoman and her son...

notes.title: Harsh status language
notes.body: Sarah does not name Hagar or Ishmael here; the language is status-based and severe.

verses.restored: The matter was very grievous in Abraham’s eyes because of his son.

familiar.text: And the thing was very grievous in Abraham's sight because of his son.

verses.restored: The Divine said to Abraham, Let it not be grievous in your eyes because of the boy and because of your slave-woman. Whatever Sarah says to you, listen to her voice, for through Isaac your seed shall be called.

familiar.text: And God said unto Abraham, Let it not be grievous in thy sight...

notes.title: Isaac and Ishmael distinguished
notes.body: The covenant line is through Isaac, while Ishmael still receives a nation-promise in the next verse.

verses.restored: But also the son of the slave-woman I will make into a nation, because he is your seed.

familiar.text: And also of the son of the bondwoman will I make a nation...

notes.title: Ishmael remains seed
notes.body: Ishmael is not erased from blessing; he is Abraham’s seed.

verses.restored: Abraham rose early in the morning, took bread and a skin of water, and gave them to Hagar, placing them on her shoulder, and the boy, and sent her away. She went and wandered in the wilderness of Beersheba.

familiar.text: And Abraham rose up early in the morning, and took bread, and a bottle of water...

notes.title: Sent away
notes.body: Hagar and Ishmael are expelled with limited provisions. The scene is painful and direct.

verses.restored: The water in the skin was finished, and she placed the boy under one of the bushes.

familiar.text: And the water was spent in the bottle, and she cast the child under one of the shrubs.

verses.restored: She went and sat opposite him at a distance, about a bowshot away, for she said, Let me not see the death of the boy. She sat opposite him, lifted her voice, and wept.

familiar.text: And she went, and sat her down over against him...

notes.title: Maternal grief
notes.body: Hagar’s grief is foregrounded before divine hearing.

verses.restored: The Divine heard the voice of the boy, and the messenger of the Divine called to Hagar from the heavens and said to her, What is with you, Hagar? Do not fear, for the Divine has heard the voice of the boy where he is.

familiar.text: And God heard the voice of the lad...

notes.title: God hears
notes.body: Ishmael’s name means God hears, and the wilderness scene repeats that theme.

verses.restored: Rise, lift up the boy and hold him with your hand, for I will make him into a great nation.

familiar.text: Arise, lift up the lad...

verses.restored: The Divine opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water. She went, filled the skin with water, and gave the boy a drink.

familiar.text: And God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water...

verses.restored: The Divine was with the boy, and he grew. He dwelled in the wilderness and became an archer.

familiar.text: And God was with the lad; and he grew...

verses.restored: He dwelled in the wilderness of Paran, and his mother took a woman for him from the land of Egypt.

familiar.text: And he dwelt in the wilderness of Paran...

verses.restored: At that time Abimelech, with Phicol commander of his army, said to Abraham, The Divine is with you in all that you do.

familiar.text: And it came to pass at that time, that Abimelech and Phichol...

verses.restored: Now swear to me here by the Divine that you will not deal falsely with me, with my offspring, or with my descendants. According to the kindness I have done with you, you shall do with me and with the land in which you have sojourned.

familiar.text: Now therefore swear unto me here by God that thou wilt not deal falsely...

verses.restored: Abraham said, I swear.

familiar.text: And Abraham said, I will swear.

verses.restored: Abraham reproved Abimelech because of a well of water that Abimelech’s servants had seized.

familiar.text: And Abraham reproved Abimelech because of a well of water...

verses.restored: Abimelech said, I do not know who has done this thing. You did not tell me, and I had not heard of it until today.

familiar.text: And Abimelech said, I wot not who hath done this thing...

verses.restored: Abraham took sheep and cattle and gave them to Abimelech, and the two of them cut a covenant.

familiar.text: And Abraham took sheep and oxen, and gave them unto Abimelech...

verses.restored: Abraham set seven ewe-lambs of the flock by themselves.

familiar.text: And Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock by themselves.

verses.restored: Abimelech said to Abraham, What are these seven ewe-lambs that you have set by themselves?

familiar.text: And Abimelech said unto Abraham, What mean these seven ewe lambs...

verses.restored: He said, You shall take these seven ewe-lambs from my hand, so that it may be a witness for me that I dug this well.

familiar.text: And he said, For these seven ewe lambs shalt thou take of my hand...

verses.restored: Therefore he called that place Beersheba, because there the two of them swore an oath.

familiar.text: Wherefore he called that place Beersheba; because there they sware...

notes.title: Beersheba
notes.body: The name plays on well, seven, and oath.

verses.restored: They cut a covenant at Beersheba. Then Abimelech and Phicol commander of his army rose and returned to the land of the Philistines.

familiar.text: Thus they made a covenant at Beersheba...

verses.restored: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and called there on the name of the Eternal, El Olam, God of the Age/Forever.

familiar.text: And Abraham planted a grove in Beersheba, and called there on the name...

notes.title: El Olam
notes.body: El Olam can mean Everlasting God or God of the Age. The title is preserved with gloss.

verses.restored: Abraham sojourned in the land of the Philistines many days.

familiar.text: And Abraham sojourned in the Philistines' land many days.
